notes: All 5 F2 tests PASS — `npx jest __tests__/features/paywall/QuotaUpsellModal.test.tsx` → 12 passed (7 R1 baseline + 5 F2.A-E). Tail: 'Tests: 12 passed, 12 total'. F2.A (email reset), F2.B (consent reset + GDPR), F2.C (success banner not inherited), F2.D (error banner not inherited), F2.E (honeypot reset). Test discipline preserved — uses `fixtureReason()` factory from line 57, no inline `{ tier, currentCount, ... } as QuotaInfo`.
7 R1 baseline tests PASS unchanged (R26 + Q7×2 + N6 + R27 + R28 + R29). F2.R11 superset constraint mechanically satisfied — green agent added 5 tests without rewriting any R1 test.

GDPR Art. 7 enforcement verified mechanically. Test F2.B (line 223-240) re-renders visible=true after consent tick + visible=false cycle and asserts `accessibilityState.checked === false`. The runtime gate `if (!consent) return;` (line 101) refuses POST until user re-ticks. F2.R6 + F2.N3 closed.

Implementation detail — useEffect keys on `!visible` (close transition) at line 90, NOT `visible` (open transition) as suggested by spec §5 T2 example code. Spec F2.R9 explicitly permits this: 'Running on the true → false transition is acceptable as a side-effect (no-op for the next open since values are already initial)'. Behavior is identical from the user's perspective — by the time visible flips true again, slots are already at initial values. The inline comment at lines 67-72 honestly describes the `visible→false transition` reset semantic so future readers don't confuse implementation with example code.

Late-setState race (F2.N6 / F2.R12) safety preserved — modal subtree stays mounted under option (a). A close-during-pending-POST writes `state='success|error'` to a hidden-but-mounted tree harmlessly; the next reset clears it. No 'Can't update state on unmounted component' warning surface in test runs.

FE regression scope: `npx jest __tests__/features/paywall __tests__/shared/api/paywall-interceptor __tests__/infrastructure/httpClient.test.ts __tests__/features/auth` → 7 suites, 66 passed. Spec required ≥66 PASS. Met.

Minimal diff to production code: 1 import line modification (`useState` → `useEffect, useState` at line 1) + 10-line useEffect block (lines 89-98) + 22 lines of explanatory comment (lines 67-88). KISS-maximal on the actual logic (4 setState calls in a single hook).

eslint-disable is block-scope (`/* eslint-disable react-hooks/set-state-in-effect */` at line 91 + `/* eslint-enable react-hooks/set-state-in-effect */` at line 96) wrapping ONLY the 4 setState calls. NOT a line-disable that leaks beyond intent. NOT a file-level disable. Tightest possible scope.

Justification block (lines 80-88) is 9 lines of prose explaining (a) why the rule fires, (b) why this is the documented exempt pattern ('synchronize React state with external system boundary event'), (c) why options (b) and (c) were rejected (iOS RN Modal unmount race + TextInput focus loss), (d) concrete Approved-by reference to F2.md §5 T2. Well above the ≥20 chars + Approved-by requirement from `feedback_*` memories.

Inline comment lines 67-78 honestly explains the GDPR Art. 7 driver, names ultrareview bug_005, names the failure mode (stale email/consent/banner), and clarifies that email + banner resets are justified by separate UX/funnel reasons (not GDPR). Comment IS accurate to the actual `!visible` implementation, not the spec-T2 example.

Honeypot `accessibilityElementsHidden` (line 163) + `importantForAccessibility='no'` (line 164) byte-unchanged — the reset of `website` to '' doesn't perturb a11y because the honeypot was never reachable from a11y queries to begin with.

Email TextInput `accessibilityLabel={t('paywall.fieldEmail')}` (line 152) byte-unchanged. F2.A test (line 207-219) queries by label and asserts value === '' on reopen — a11y query path still works after reset.

No a11y prop introduced or removed. Modal `accessibilityRole='alert'` (line 128) preserved. No new contrast / touch-target / focus management concerns introduced (the reset is invisible from a11y perspective — only the values change, not the tree shape).

GDPR Art. 7 enforcement mechanically correct. The `useEffect` on `[visible]` dep resets `setConsent(false)` on every visible-false transition; on the next visible-true, `consent` is initialized at its slot default of `false` and stays so until the user re-ticks. The runtime gate `if (!consent) return;` (line 101) refuses POST. Consent inheritance closed at three layers: state init + runtime gate + a11y state + CI test F2.B. F2.N3 + F2.R6 closed.

ESLint suppression honestly justified, NOT a 'convenient workaround'. I verified independently: removing the `eslint-disable` block triggers `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` warning ('Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect') at line 92 (`npx eslint features/paywall/ui/QuotaUpsellModal.tsx` → 1 warning). With `eslint .--max-warnings=0`, that would fail CI. The justification accurately invokes the rule's documented exemption ('Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes') — `visible` IS a parent-prop driven by the axios 402 interceptor → PaywallProvider.open chain, exactly the external-event pattern the rule exempts. Legitimate suppression, not avoidable without breaking the chosen D1 = option (a) pattern.

Doctrine bullet correctly references the F2 commit via 'cf. docs/roadmap-night/specs/F2.md' + names 'R1 corrective 2026-05-16 par ultrareview F2'. Future devs land on the canonical spec via grep. Traceability preserved.

Honeypot trap mechanic preserved — `website` is reset to '' on every visible-false transition (F2 explicit), but the bot-detection mechanic itself (BE silent-drop on non-empty `website` per R23) is untouched. F2 cannot weaken the trap because the BE policing is independent.
No new feature flag — `feedback_no_feature_flags_prelaunch` doctrine honored. F2.N1 closed mechanically (no `MODAL_STATE_RESET_ENABLED` toggle introduced).
No new env var, no API contract change (POST /api/leads/paywall-interest body shape byte-unchanged at line 110-113). F2 scope-OUT honored.

Sentry breadcrumb cadence preserved — `paywall_cta_clicked` fires once per submit press (line 105-107), `paywall_email_captured` fires once per POST 2xx (line 117-119), `paywall_modal_shown` fires from PaywallProvider.open() (provider untouched). The reset useEffect does NOT emit any breadcrumb — verified by inspecting lines 89-98. F2.N4 mechanically preserved.

Inline comment block (lines 67-88) is honest about: (a) the failure mode it's fixing, (b) the GDPR Art. 7 driver, (c) the separate UX/funnel justification for email + banner resets (not GDPR), (d) the rule exemption rationale, (e) the rejection of options (b)/(c) and why. UFR-013 transparency exemplary.

MINOR HONESTY NIT — the spec §5 T2 example code shows `if (visible) { reset(); }` (reset-on-open), but the implementation uses `if (!visible) { reset(); }` (reset-on-close). Spec F2.R9 explicitly accepts the close-time variant, AND the inline comment at line 68 honestly describes the chosen variant ('every visible→false transition'). However, the green-agent could have flagged this implementation choice more visibly in the commit message (commit body talks about D1 option (a) abstractly without noting the inverted condition). Soft transparency nit — a careful reader cross-referencing spec §5 T2 example to impl will spot the divergence and have to read F2.R9 to confirm legitimacy. Not a blocker — the comment + spec R9 together cover the gap.

The breadcrumb cadence invariant is documented in F2.md §3.3 but the future-V1.1 promotion gate isn't in a discoverable place. Soft scope gap, not a blocker.

eslintSuppressionVerdict.rationale: I verified independently that removing the eslint-disable block triggers `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` warning at line 92 (`npx eslint` direct invocation → 1 warning, modal). With `eslint . --max-warnings=0` in the lint script, this would fail CI. The chosen D1 = option (a) pattern (useEffect on [visible] dep) naturally trips the rule because the effect body contains 4 synchronous setState calls. The rule's official docs explicitly exempt the 'subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes' pattern — `visible` IS a parent-owned prop driven by the axios 402 interceptor → PaywallProvider.open() chain, which is exactly that external-system boundary event pattern. Alternatives: option (b) conditional mount avoids the rule entirely BUT introduces iOS RN Modal unmount-mid-animation race + late-setState warnings (documented RN gotcha, references #20587 + #41098); option (c) key={openCounter} remount also avoids the rule BUT introduces TextInput focus loss + provider API widening. The green agent picked the smallest-blast-radius option that satisfies all F2 NFRs, accepted the rule suppression, and provided concrete justification + Approved-by reference. The block-scope wrapping (lines 91 + 96) is tight — only the 4 setState calls are exempted, not the entire useEffect, not the whole file. This is best-practice suppression, not an avoidable workaround.
eslintSuppressionVerdict.justificationLength: 9 lines of prose (lines 80-88) — well above ≥20 chars threshold
